info.title: Sitewire API
info.description:
The Sitewire API provides programmatic access to site inspection and draw management workflows.

## Authentication
API requests require authentication using token authentication headers. Authorized users can create and delete credentials in the API tab.
- `access-token`: Authentication token, e.g. `NNAU6z8DMhFCsYOtT3uqCA`
- `client`: Client identifier, e.g. `C_m-s_mM0jSw8PjFqxrS1c`
- `uid`: User identifier, e.g. `name@example.com`

tags.name: Borrowers
tags.description: Borrower organizations represent real estate development teams. Borrowers submit draw requests and site checks to lenders for review.

tags.name: Lenders
tags.description: Lender organizations oversee loan-financed projects. Lenders enter project budgets, set funding parameters, review borrower-submitted draw requests and site checks, forward requests to capital partners, and manage the overall funding process.

tags.name: Capital Partners
tags.description: Capital partner organizations can access their portfolio and participate in the draw review process.

tags.name: Properties
tags.description: Properties represent construction projects. Properties may have a lender, borrower,  and capital partner. Properties consolidate loan, site check, documents, and other details. Properties are the central entity that connect all other resources in the system.

tags.name: Budgets
tags.description: Budgets (also referred to as loans) represent the construction project budget. Each budget contains job items (budget line items) that define the scope of work and available funds. They also define funding parameters like funding threshold or ratio. Draw requests reference job items from the property's loan budget.

tags.name: Draws
tags.description: Draws represent funding requests for construction projects. Each draw contains one or more dollar-denominated requests against budget job items. Draws go through a workflow from borrower submission through some combination of inspector, lender, and capital partner approval. Draws belong to a property's loan budget and can include inspections, documents, and other details.

tags.name: Requests
tags.description: Requests represent individual job item requests within a draw. Each request links a job item to media documenting the work and dollar-denominated requested and approved amounts, among other details. Requests are the building blocks of draws and represent specific work items being funded.

tags.name: Deliverable Updates
tags.description: Deliverable updates enable Site Checks, condition reports with no funding component.  Each deliverable update contains multiple deliverable update entries and goes through a review workflow.  Deliverable updates include proofs (photos/videos), documents, and a PDF report, among other details.

tags.name: Deliverable Update Entries
tags.description: Deliverable update entries represent individual deliverable items within a deliverable update. Each entry links a deliverable item to a deliverable update and can include proofs (photos/videos) and notes.

tags.name: Quick Notify Statuses
tags.description: Quick notify statuses are lender-defined pipeline labels that can be attached to draws to communicate where a draw sits in the lender's internal workflow (e.g. "Awaiting internal approval", "Sent to the wire department").

Invalid address.description: Addresses must validate through the Google Geocoding API. Addresses may be fuzzy-matched to valid results using the Google Place Autocomplete API. If a property address cannot be validated due to out-of-date or missing maps data, please contact Sitewire customer support for assistance.

errors: End date must be after the start date

allow_reallocation.description: Whether job item budget reallocation is allowed during draw processing

capital_partner_id.description: Unique identifier for the capital partner company associated with this property. Capital partners provide funding alongside lenders.

default_draw_coordinator_id.description: Unique identifier for the lender user who will coordinate draws for this property by default. Must be a lender_owner or lender_viewer role.

draw_checklist_template_id.description: Unique identifier for the checklist template to use for draws on this property

borrower_entity_name.description: Legal entity name of the borrower

processing_fee.description: DEPRECATED: Use processing_fee_cents instead. Processing fee as a decimal dollar amount (e.g. 100.56 or '100.56'). Accepts numeric or string dollar amounts.

processing_fee_cents.description: Processing fee amount in US cents

end_date.description: Project end date (YYYY-MM-DD). Must be after start_date.

inspection_method.description: Inspection method for the property. `mobile` enables virtual inspections using the Sitewire mobile app. `traditional` implies the use of a third-party field inspector for onsite inspections.

loan_number.description: Lender's loan or project reference number

other_fees.description: DEPRECATED: Use other_fees_cents instead. Other fees as a decimal dollar amount (e.g. 7.50 or '7.50'). Accepts numeric or string dollar amounts.

other_fees_cents.description: Other fees amount in US cents

project_description.description: Detailed description of the construction project scope and objectives
project_description.example: Complete renovation of single-family home including kitchen remodel and bathroom updates

project_id.description: External project identifier or reference number

require_capital_partner_approval.description: Whether capital partner approval is required before draws can be funded

require_sitewire_inspector.description: Whether a Sitewire inspector is required to approve draws before they reach the lender. Only applies to draws with an `inspection_method` of `mobile`. Set this to `false` to complete virtual-inspected draws using in-house review.

start_date.description: Project start date (YYYY-MM-DD). Required for all properties.

total_units.description: Total number of units in the development. Required for multi_family_residential. Optional for commercial and other. Must be absent for single_family_residential.

money_transfer_fee.description: DEPRECATED: Use money_transfer_fee_cents instead. Money transfer fee as a decimal dollar amount (e.g. 25.00 or '25.00'). Accepts numeric or string dollar amounts.

money_transfer_fee_cents.description: Money transfer fee amount in US cents

address.description: Property address. Must be a valid address that can be geocoded via Google Maps API.

lender_comments.description: Comments from the lender about this funding request. Used to provide feedback or explanation about approval decisions.

approved_cents.description: Approved amount in cents. For pending draws, this is applied as pending approved.

Address.description: Physical address with geocoding support. Addresses are validated and geocoded via Google Maps API.

full_address.description: Complete formatted address (read-only, generated by system)

street_with_unit.description: Street address with unit if applicable (read-only, generated by system)

csz.description: City, State ZIP formatted (read-only, generated by system)

Borrower.description:
Borrower company entity representing construction companies or developers that own and develop properties.
Each borrower can have multiple properties and works with lenders and capital partners to fund construction projects.
Borrowers submit draw requests for funding and manage property development workflows.

id.description: Unique identifier for the borrower company

company_name.description: Legal name of the borrower company

contact_first_name.description: First name of primary contact person

contact_last_name.description: Last name of primary contact person

contact_email.description: Email address of primary contact person

contact_phone.description: Phone number of primary contact person

Budget.description:
Budget (also referred to as loan) entity representing the financial plan for a property.
Each budget contains job items (budget line items) that define the scope of work and allocated funds.
Budgets are associated with a single property and control funding parameters like funding ratios and thresholds.
All draws and requests must reference job items from a property's budget.

draw_eligible.description: Whether draws can be created for this property's budget

funding_ratio.description: Percentage of approved amounts that the lender will fund (0-100). Always numeric - 100.0 represents standard funding (100% coverage).

funding_threshold_cents.description: Funding threshold in US cents - amount the borrower must fund first before lender funding begins. Always numeric - 0 means no threshold.

total_budgeted_cents.description: Total budgeted amount across all job items, in US cents. Read-only; derived from job items.

total_approved_cents.description: Sum of approved amounts across all requests on this budget's draws, in US cents. Read-only.

balance_cents.description: Remaining budget: total_budgeted_cents minus total_approved_cents, in US cents. Read-only.

job_items.description: Budget line items (job items) in this budget

BudgetPartial.description:
Budget (also referred to as loan) entity representing the financial plan for a property.
This is a partial budget view used in property responses, without job items.
Budgets are associated with a single property and control funding parameters like funding ratios and thresholds.

draw_eligible.description: Whether draws can be created for this property's budget

funding_ratio.description: Percentage of approved amounts that the lender will fund (0-100). Always numeric - 100.0 represents standard funding (100% coverage).

funding_threshold_cents.description: Funding threshold in US cents - amount the borrower must fund first before lender funding begins. Always numeric - 0 means no threshold.

CapitalPartner.description:
Capital partner company entity providing additional funding alongside lenders for construction projects.
Capital partners can be associated with properties through company roles and participate in the draw approval workflow,
particularly for draws in pending_capital_partner status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the capital partner company

name.description: Company name of the capital partner

ChecklistTemplate.description: Checklist template defining required items and documentation for draws or properties.

id.description: Unique identifier for the checklist template

is_default.description: Whether this is the default template for new properties

Draw.description:
Draw entity representing a funding request for a construction project.
Each draw contains multiple requests for budget line items (job items) and goes through a workflow
from borrower submission to lender/capital partner approval.
Draws belong to a property's budget (loan) and can include inspections, documents, events, and PDFs.
Use draws to track the funding lifecycle of a construction project.

number.description: Sequential draw number for the property

historical.description: Whether this is a historical draw (created before property was added to Sitewire)

status.description: Current status of the draw in the workflow

funding_ratio.description: Percentage of approved amounts that the lender will fund (0-100). Always numeric - 100.0 represents standard funding (100% coverage).

funding_threshold_cents.description: Cumulative funding threshold in US cents across all job items for this draw. Amount the borrower must fund first before lender funding begins. Always numeric - 0 means no threshold.

coordinator_id.description: Unique identifier for the lender user coordinating this draw

quick_notify_status_id.description: Identifier of the lender-defined quick notify status currently set on this draw. Nil when no status is set. See GET /api/v2/quick_notify_statuses for available ids.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the draw was last updated. Note: status transitions update this field.

total_requested_cents.description: Sum of requested_cents across all requests in this draw, in US cents

total_approved_cents.description: Sum of approved_cents across all requests in this draw, in US cents

requests.description: Budget line item funding requests included in this draw

draw_events.description: History of events for this draw

pdf_src.description: URL to the draw PDF document, if generated. There will be a delay between draw events and updated PDF availability.

DrawEvent.description: Event in a draw's history tracking status changes and actions

actor.description: User or system that triggered the event

id.description: Unique identifier for the actor user (null for system events)

occurred_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the event occurred

DrawSummary.description: Draw entity for list endpoints. Excludes property, requests, draw_events, and pdf_src which are only in show endpoint.

number.description: Sequential draw number for the property

historical.description: Whether this is a historical draw (created before property was added to Sitewire)

status.description: Current status of the draw in the workflow

funding_ratio.description: Percentage of approved amounts that the lender will fund (0-100). Always numeric - 100.0 represents standard funding (100% coverage).

funding_threshold_cents.description: Cumulative funding threshold in US cents across all job items for this draw. Amount the borrower must fund first before lender funding begins. Always numeric - 0 means no threshold.

coordinator_id.description: Unique identifier for the lender user coordinating this draw

quick_notify_status_id.description: Identifier of the lender-defined quick notify status currently set on this draw. Nil when no status is set. See GET /api/v2/quick_notify_statuses for available ids.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the draw was last updated. Note: status transitions update this field.

total_requested_cents.description: Sum of requested_cents across all requests in this draw, in US cents

total_approved_cents.description: Sum of approved_cents across all requests in this draw, in US cents

JobItem.description:
Job item (budget line item) entity representing a specific work item in a property's budget.
Each job item has a budgeted amount and tracks approved and released amounts through draws.
Job items are required for creating draw requests.

id.description: Unique identifier for the job item

name.description: Name of the job item (work item description)

description.description: Detailed description of the work item
description.example: Install custom kitchen cabinets with soft-close hinges

budgeted_cents.description: Budgeted amount for this job item in US cents

available_cents.description: Available budget remaining for this job item in US cents (read-only)

total_approved_cents.description: Total amount approved across all draws for this job item in US cents (read-only)

total_released_cents.description: Total amount released (funded) for this job item in US cents (read-only)

required_image_count.description: Minimum number of photos required for draw requests on this job item. Always >= 0 in API responses.

required_image_status.description: Proof status for photos: not-accepted (hidden), accepted (optional), required (minimum count enforced)

required_video_count.description: Minimum number of videos required for draw requests on this job item. Always >= 0 in API responses.

required_video_status.description: Proof status for videos: not-accepted (hidden), accepted (optional), required (minimum count enforced)

required_document_status.description: Proof status for documents: not-accepted (hidden), accepted (optional), required (minimum count enforced)

mandatory.description: Whether this job item must be included in all draws. When true, draws cannot be created without this job item.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the job item was created

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the job item was last updated

Lender.description:
Lender company entity providing primary funding for construction projects.
Lenders approve draws, set funding parameters (ratios and thresholds), and coordinate the funding process.
Each lender can fund multiple properties and works with borrowers and capital partners to manage construction financing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the lender company

email.description: Primary contact email for the lender company

allow_overage.description: Whether this lender allows draw requests to exceed budgeted amounts

lender_budget.description: DEPRECATED: Legacy funding percentage (0-100). Use default_funding_ratio instead. This field will be removed in v3.0.

default_funding_ratio.description: Default percentage of approved amounts this lender funds (0-100)

default_funding_threshold_cents.description: Default cumulative threshold in US cents across all job items.

checklist_templates.description: Draw checklist templates available for this lender (only included in show endpoint)

capital_partners.description: Capital partner companies available to work with this lender (only included in show endpoint)

users.description: Users associated with this lender company (only included in show endpoint)

deleted.description: Whether this user has been soft-deleted

LenderSummary.description: Lender entity for list endpoints. Excludes checklist_templates, capital_partners, and users which are only in show endpoint.

id.description: Unique identifier for the lender company

email.description: Primary contact email for the lender company

allow_overage.description: Whether this lender allows draw requests to exceed budgeted amounts

lender_budget.description: DEPRECATED: Legacy funding percentage (0-100). Use default_funding_ratio instead. This field will be removed in v3.0.

default_funding_ratio.description: Default percentage of approved amounts this lender funds (0-100)

default_funding_threshold_cents.description: Default cumulative threshold in US cents across all job items.

MoneyAmount.description: Monetary amount in a specific currency. All amounts are stored in the smallest currency unit (cents for USD).

cents.description: Amount in smallest currency unit (cents for USD)

Property.description:
Property entity representing a construction project or real estate development.
Each property belongs to a borrower and lender, and may have capital partners.
Properties contain budgets (loans) with job items, draws for funding requests, and deliverable updates for site checks.
Properties are the central entity that connects all other resources in the system.

project_description.description: Detailed description of the construction project scope and objectives
project_description.example: Complete renovation of single-family home including kitchen remodel and bathroom updates

end_date.description: Project end date (YYYY-MM-DD). Must be after start_date.

total_units.description: Total number of units in the development. Required for multi_family_residential. Optional for commercial and other. Must be absent for single_family_residential.

loan_number.description: Lender's loan or project reference number

borrower_entity_name.description: Legal entity name of the borrower

inspection_method.description: Inspection method for the property. 'mobile' uses the Sitewire mobile app for virtual inspections (may require Sitewire inspector review). 'traditional' uses preferred field inspectors for on-site inspections.

require_sitewire_inspector.description: Whether a Sitewire inspector is required to review virtual inspections. Only applies when inspection_method is 'mobile'. Set to false to allow virtual inspections without Sitewire inspector review.

require_capital_partner_approval.description: Whether capital partner approval is required before draws can be funded

allow_reallocation.description: Whether job item budget reallocation is allowed during draw processing

inactive.description: Whether the property is marked as inactive

default_draw_coordinator_id.description: Unique identifier for the lender user who coordinates draws by default

draw_checklist_template_id.description: Unique identifier for the checklist template to use for draws

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the property was created

borrower.description: Borrower company associated with this property (simplified object)

lender.description: Lender company associated with this property (simplified object)

capital_partner.description: Capital partner company associated with this property

id.description: Unique identifier for the capital partner company

name.description: Company name of the capital partner

processing_fee.description: DEPRECATED: Use processing_fee_cents instead. Processing fee as a decimal dollar string (e.g. '100.56').

processing_fee_cents.description: Processing fee amount in US cents

money_transfer_fee.description: DEPRECATED: Use money_transfer_fee_cents instead. Money transfer fee as a decimal dollar string (e.g. '25.00').

money_transfer_fee_cents.description: Money transfer fee amount in US cents

other_fees.description: DEPRECATED: Use other_fees_cents instead. Other fees as a decimal dollar string (e.g. '7.50').

other_fees_cents.description: Other fees amount in US cents

budget.description: Budget information (partial view without job items, used in property responses)

deliverables.description: Deliverables associated with this property (only included in show endpoint)

status.description: Status of the deliverable update in the workflow

documents.description: Documents associated with this property (only included in show endpoint)

PropertyBorrowerAssignmentResponse.description: Response from assigning a borrower to a property

contact_email.description: Email address of the assigned borrower

PropertyBorrowerSummary.description: Simplified borrower information in property list responses

PropertyBudgetSummary.description: Simplified budget information in property list responses

draw_eligible.description: Whether draws can be created for this property's budget

PropertyLenderSummary.description: Simplified lender information in property list responses

PropertySummary.description: Property entity for list endpoints. Uses simplified nested objects and excludes full budget with draws, deliverables, and documents which are only in show endpoint.

borrower_entity_name.description: Legal entity name of the borrower

loan_number.description: Lender's loan or project reference number

total_units.description: Total number of units in the development

project_description.description: Detailed description of the construction project scope and objectives

inactive.description: Whether the property is marked as inactive

default_draw_coordinator_id.description: Unique identifier for the lender user who coordinates draws by default

allow_reallocation.description: Whether job item budget reallocation is allowed during draw processing

draw_checklist_template_id.description: Unique identifier for the checklist template to use for draws

require_capital_partner_approval.description: Whether capital partner approval is required before draws can be funded

require_sitewire_inspector.description: Whether a Sitewire inspector is required to review virtual inspections

processing_fee.description: DEPRECATED: Use processing_fee_cents instead. Processing fee as a decimal dollar string (e.g. '100.56').

processing_fee_cents.description: Processing fee amount in US cents

money_transfer_fee.description: DEPRECATED: Use money_transfer_fee_cents instead. Money transfer fee as a decimal dollar string (e.g. '25.00').

money_transfer_fee_cents.description: Money transfer fee amount in US cents

other_fees.description: DEPRECATED: Use other_fees_cents instead. Other fees as a decimal dollar string (e.g. '7.50').

other_fees_cents.description: Other fees amount in US cents

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the property was created

QuickNotifyStatus.description: Lender-defined pipeline label assigned to a draw to communicate where it sits in the lender's internal workflow (e.g. "Awaiting internal approval").

id.description: Unique identifier for the quick notify status

lender_id.description: ID of the lender this status belongs to

Request.description:
Request entity representing an individual budget line item funding request within a draw.
Each request links a job item (from the property's budget) to a draw and tracks requested and approved amounts.
Requests can include inspections (photos/videos), lender comments, and inspector comments.
Requests are the building blocks of draws and represent specific work items being funded.

requested_cents.description: Amount requested by borrower in US cents

approved_cents.description: Amount approved by lender in US cents

job_item.description: Budget line item this request is for

lender_comments.description: Comments from the lender about this funding request

inspector_comments.description: Comments from the inspector about this funding request

ValidationError.description: Validation error response. May contain field-specific errors (object) or general error messages (array).
